meta.description: Smart contract infrastructure: proxies, reentrancy, storage, bridges
meta.usage: Use patterns[].lineStart/lineEnd to read exact sections

patterns.title: Proxy Pattern Implementation Vulnerabilities
patterns.rootCause: Proxy pattern vulnerabilities stem from the separation of contract logic (implementation) and state (proxy), combined with the use of delegatecall for forwarding calls. This architectural pattern crea

patterns.title: Pattern 1: Missing disableInitializers in Implementation Constructor
patterns.rootCause: Implementation contract constructor doesn't disable initializers

patterns.title: Pattern 2: Implementation Destruction via Selfdestruct
patterns.rootCause: Anyone can destroy implementation contract

patterns.title: Pattern 3: Unprotected UUPS Upgrade Function
patterns.rootCause: Anyone can upgrade the contract

patterns.title: Pattern 4: No Storage Gap in Upgradeable Base Contract
patterns.rootCause: Base contract has no storage gap

patterns.title: Pattern 5: Storage Collision - Non-EIP1967 Implementation Slot
patterns.rootCause: Implementation address stored at predictable slot

patterns.title: Pattern 6: Initialization Front-Running
patterns.rootCause: Two-step deployment allows front-running

patterns.title: Pattern 7: Wrong Ownable Library in Upgradeable Contract
patterns.rootCause: Using non-upgradeable Ownable in proxy pattern

patterns.title: Pattern 8: Delegatecall Nonce Reset Allowing Re-initialization
patterns.rootCause: Delegatecall can reset nonce variable

patterns.title: Pattern 9: Constructor State Not Available in Proxy
patterns.rootCause: Constructor sets state, but proxy never sees it

patterns.title: Pattern 10: Clone Validation Checking Insufficient Bytes
patterns.rootCause: Only checks first 54 bytes of clone

patterns.title: Pattern 11: Metamorphic Implementation Contract Risk
patterns.rootCause: Implementation deployed via CREATE2 can be replaced

patterns.title: Pattern 12: Plugin Selector Collision Override
patterns.rootCause: Plugin selectors can collide and override each other

patterns.title: Pattern 13: Persisted msg.value in Batch Delegatecalls
patterns.rootCause: msg.value reused across multiple delegatecalls

patterns.title: Pattern 14: Module Upgrade Without State Migration
patterns.rootCause: Module upgrade doesn't migrate state or assets

patterns.title: Pattern 15: Storage Layout Change on Upgrade Breaks Roles
searchKeywords: storage layout change upgrade breaks roles
patterns.rootCause: Upgrade changes storage layout, corrupting access control

patterns.title: Pattern 16: Missing Contract Existence Check Before Delegatecall
searchKeywords: missing contract existence check before delegatecall
patterns.rootCause: Delegatecall to address with no code silently succeeds

patterns.title: Pattern 17: Proxy Shadowing Implementation Functions
patterns.rootCause: Proxy has public methods that shadow implementation

patterns.title: Pattern 18: Permissions/Allowances Not Reset on Proxy Ownership Transfer
patterns.rootCause: Permissions and allowances persist after ownership transfer

patterns.title: Pattern 19: Temporary Owner Change During Delegatecall
patterns.rootCause: Owner can be changed during delegatecall execution

patterns.title: Pattern 20: Proxy Reuse Without Implementation Version Check
patterns.rootCause: Reusing old proxy clones without checking implementation version

patterns.title: Pattern 21: CREATE2 Salt Predictability/Front-Running
patterns.rootCause: CREATE2 salt based only on tx.origin, not owner

patterns.title: Pattern 22: Counterfactual Wallet Deployment Hijacking
patterns.rootCause: Entrypoint not included in address generation

patterns.title: Pattern 23: Variable Initialization at Declaration in Upgradeable Contract
patterns.rootCause: Variables initialized at declaration, not in initialize()

patterns.title: Pattern 24: Beacon Implementation Without Contract Existence Check
patterns.rootCause: Beacon doesn't verify implementation exists

patterns.title: Pattern 25: Approval Set in Constructor for Proxy Contract
patterns.rootCause: Approval set in constructor, not available to proxy

patterns.title: Pattern 26: Proxy Cannot Redeploy After Selfdestruct
patterns.rootCause: Destroyed proxy address permanently blocked in registry

patterns.title: Pattern 27: Whitelist Bypass via Upgradeable Proxy
patterns.rootCause: Whitelisted proxy can be upgraded to malicious implementation

patterns.title: Pattern 28: Re-Initializable Proxy Due to Missing Modifier
patterns.rootCause: Initialize can be called multiple times

patterns.title: Example 1: Telcoin Storage Collision [CRITICAL]
patterns.rootCause: Proxy upgrade caused storage collision

patterns.title: Example 2: EFVault Storage Collision [CRITICAL]
patterns.rootCause: Storage collision between proxy and implementation

patterns.title: Example 3: Audius Governance Storage Collision [CRITICAL]
patterns.rootCause: Governance proxy storage collision allowed malicious proposal

patterns.title: Example 4: Unstructured Storage Errors [HIGH]
patterns.rootCause: Incorrect EIP-1967 implementation

patterns.title: Example 5: Diamond Storage Collision [HIGH]
patterns.rootCause: Facets with overlapping storage

patterns.title: 2. Flash Loan Callback Reentrancy — Deposit-in-Callback

patterns.title: 5. Native Token Receive() Reentrancy (StarsArena $3M)

patterns.title: 2. Empty Market Phantom Collateral Attack

patterns.title: 1. isContract/extcodesize Logic Broken
patterns.rootCause: Smart contracts commonly use `isContract()` checks to determine whether to verify signatures using ECDSA (for EOAs) or EIP-1271 `isValidSignature()` (for contracts). EIP-7702 allows EOAs to temporaril

patterns.title: 2. Reentrancy via EIP-7702 Delegated EOAs
patterns.rootCause: Protocols that detect EIP-7702 delegated wallets may use low-level `.call` with unlimited gas to send ETH, enabling complex operations in the recipient's `receive`/`fallback` functions. This opens ree

patterns.title: 3. Missing Token Receiver Callbacks
patterns.rootCause: EIP-7702 smart wallet implementations that don't implement `IERC721Receiver` and `IERC1155Receiver` interfaces will fail to receive NFTs via `safeTransfer` functions. Since the delegated code makes th

patterns.title: 4. Cross-Chain Address Mismatch
patterns.rootCause: Smart contract wallets are deployed using factory contracts with chain-specific parameters (deployment salts, nonces, factory addresses). The same user's wallet logic results in different contract add

patterns.title: 5. tx.origin Exploitation with Smart Wallets
patterns.rootCause: Smart wallets like Gnosis Safe accept signed transactions via `execTransaction()` and execute them on behalf of signers. The function is agnostic to who calls it, as long as signatures are valid. When

patterns.title: 6. Fallback/Receive Function Issues
patterns.rootCause: EIP-7702 transforms an EOA into what appears to be a smart contract. If the delegated contract's `fallback` function lacks the `payable` modifier, ETH transfers using `.call{value: x}("")` will fail,

patterns.title: 7. Session Key Impersonation in Smart Wallets
patterns.rootCause: When a smart wallet allows multiple active sessions concurrently, the signature validation must ensure that the session key that signed the transaction is the same one being used for the operation. Wi

patterns.title: 8. NFT Permit Function Exploitation
